message.content: Implement the following plan: # @Mentions Feature ## Context Users need a way to mention other users in their posts. Mentions are limited to people you follow, which prevents abuse and keeps autocomplete relevant. When mentioned, users receive a notification. Mentions are clickable links to profiles on both web and mobile. ## Implementation Steps ### Step 1: Backend — Search Following Users Endpoint New endpoint so the autocomplete can search among people the author follows. **`server/services/getService.js`** — Add `searchFollowingUsersService(userId, query, limit)`: - Query `follows` table for `following_id` where `follower_id = userId` - If no query: return all followed users (up to limit), ordered by name - If query: ILIKE search on `name` and `username` within followed user IDs, deduplicate - Returns `{ data: [{ id, name, username, image_url, badge }] }` **`server/controller/getController.js`** — Add `searchFollowingUsersController` **`server/routes/routes.js`** — Add `GET /users/following/search` (requireAuth) **`client/API/Api.js`** — Add `searchFollowingUsers(token, query, limit)` ### Step 2: MentionNode (Custom Lexical DecoratorNode) Follow the exact `ImageNode.jsx` pattern but inline. **Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionNode.jsx`**: - `DecoratorNode` subclass, type = `"mention"` - Properties: `__mentionName`, `__mentionUserId`, `__mentionUsername` - `isInline()` returns `true` (critical difference from ImageNode) - `exportJSON()` → `{ type: "mention", version: 1, mentionName, mentionUserId, mentionUsername }` - `importJSON()` → reconstructs node from serialized data - `getTextContent()` → `@username` (for word count, copy/paste) - `decorate()` → renders `` with profile navigation - `createDOM()` → `` - Export helpers: `$createMentionNode()`, `$isMentionNode()` **Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionComponent.jsx`**: - Renders `@{name}` - Click navigates to profile using `handleClickProfile` from `client/helpers/handleClicks.js` - When `isEditable=true` (in editor), clicks do nothing (no navigation while editing) ### Step 3: Register MentionNode **`client/src/App.jsx`** (line 75): Add `MentionNode` to nodes array **`client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx`** (lines 412, 588): Add `MentionNode` to both read-only LexicalComposer nodes arrays ### Step 4: MentionPlugin (Autocomplete) **Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/Plugins/MentionPlugin.jsx`**: - Uses `useLexicalComposerContext` (same pattern as `ImagePlugin.jsx`) - `registerUpdateListener` detects `@` trigger via regex `/@([\w-]*)$/` on text before cursor - Tracks query string after `@`, debounces API calls (200ms) - Calls `searchFollowingUsers` API to fetch matching followed users - Renders positioned dropdown (fixed position, calculated from DOM selection range) - Keyboard navigation: Arrow up/down, Enter/Tab to select, Escape to dismiss - On selection: replaces `@query` text with `MentionNode`, adds trailing space - `onMouseDown` with `preventDefault` on dropdown items to prevent editor blur **`client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/RichTextEditor.jsx`** (line 131): Add `` after `` ### Step 5: Mention Styles **`client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editor.css`** — Add: - `.mention-chip`: inline, accent color, font-weight 600, subtle hover bg - `.mention-chip--editing`: cursor default, lighter bg, no hover underline - `.mention-dropdown`: fixed position, z-index 200, glass bg, rounded, shadow, max-height 280px with overflow scroll - `.mention-dropdown-item`: flex row with avatar + name/username, hover highlight - `.mention-dropdown-item--selected`: highlighted state for keyboard navigation - Uses existing CSS vars (`--accent-purple`, `--bg-elevated`, `--border-light`, `--text-primary`, `--text-muted`) ### Step 6: Mention Notifications on Publish **Create `server/utils/extractMentions.js`** — `extractMentionUserIds(contentJson)`: - Walks Lexical JSON tree recursively - Collects unique `mentionUserId` from nodes with `type === 'mention'` - Returns array of user IDs **`server/services/uploadService.js`** — Modify `uploadJournalContentService` (line 293): - Change insert to `.select('id').single()` to get the new journal ID back - After successful insert, call `extractMentionUserIds(content)` - Batch insert notifications with `type: 'mention'`, `journal_id`, `sender_id`, `receiver_id` - Filter out self-mentions, cap at 50 notifications, non-fatal (try/catch) **`client/helpers/formatNoficationType.js`** — Add to `notificationTypeMap`: - `mention: 'Mentioned you in a post'` **`client/src/components/Notifications/notificationsCards.jsx`** — Add click handler for mention notifications: - Navigate to the post (same as like/comment behavior, using `journal_id`) ### Step 7: Mobile LexicalRenderer **`iskrib_mobile_app/apps/mobile/src/lib/content/LexicalRenderer.tsx`**: - Add `mentionName`, `mentionUserId`, `mentionUsername` to `LexicalNode` interface - Add `'mention'` case in `RenderInline`: render as `` with `accentAmber` color, `onPress` navigates to `VisitProfile` screen via `useNavigation` - Import `useNavigation` from `@react-navigation/native` ## Files to Create - `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionNode.jsx` - `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionComponent.jsx` - `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/Plugins/MentionPlugin.jsx` - `server/utils/extractMentions.js` ## Files to Modify - `server/services/getService.js` — add searchFollowingUsersService - `server/controller/getController.js` — add searchFollowingUsersController - `server/routes/routes.js` — add GET /users/following/search - `client/API/Api.js` — add searchFollowingUsers() - `client/src/App.jsx` — register MentionNode (line 75) - `client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx` — register MentionNode (lines 412, 588) - `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/RichTextEditor.jsx` — add MentionPlugin (line 131) - `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editor.css` — mention styles - `server/services/uploadService.js` — extract mentions + create notifications on publish - `client/helpers/formatNoficationType.js` — add 'mention' type text - `client/src/components/Notifications/notificationsCards.jsx` — mention notification click handler - `iskrib_mobile_app/apps/mobile/src/lib/content/LexicalRenderer.tsx` — mention rendering ## Key Reuse - `ImageNode.jsx` pattern → MentionNode structure (DecoratorNode, exportJSON/importJSON, decorate) - `ImagePlugin.jsx` pattern → MentionPlugin structure (useLexicalComposerContext) - `handleClickProfile` from `client/helpers/handleClicks.js` → profile navigation in MentionComponent - `searchUsersService` pattern from `server/services/getService.js` → searchFollowingUsersService (ILIKE search, dedup) - Existing notification insert pattern from `interactService.js` → mention notifications - `FormatNotificationType` map → add 'mention' entry ## Verification 1. Type `@` in editor → dropdown appears with followed users 2. Type a query → dropdown filters results 3. Arrow keys + Enter to select → MentionNode inserted inline 4. Save post → JSON contains `"type": "mention"` nodes with userId/username 5. View post in ContentView → mention renders as clickable link, navigates to `/@username` 6. Mentioned user receives notification with "Mentioned you in a post" 7. Click mention notification → navigates to the post 8. Mobile app → mention renders as tappable amber text, tapping navigates to VisitProfile If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3\f91d79f4-82df-44b3-b24f-ae7d73e41854.jsonl

content.thinking: The user wants to know how to test the @mentions feature. Let me give them a practical testing checklist based on what was implemented.

content.text:
Here's a practical testing flow:

## 1. Backend first

Start your server and test the new endpoint directly:

```bash
# In your browser or Postman, hit the search endpoint with your auth token:
GET /api/users/following/search?query=&limit=5
# Should return people you follow

GET /api/users/following/search?query=john&limit=5
# Should return followed users matching "john"
```

## 2. Editor autocomplete (main test)

1. Log in and open the editor (write a new post)
2. Type some text, then type `@` — the dropdown should appear with people you follow
3. Type a few characters after `@` — the dropdown should filter
4. Use **Arrow keys** to navigate, **Enter/Tab** to select, **Escape** to dismiss
5. After selecting, you should see a purple `@Name` chip inline in the editor with a trailing space
6. Type more text around it — the mention should stay inline and not break the flow

## 3. Save and view

1. Give the post a title, write some content with a mention, and hit **Share**
2. Open the post in ContentView — the mention should render as a clickable purple `@Name`
3. Click it — should navigate to that user's profile

## 4. Notifications

1. After publishing a post that mentions someone, check your **Supabase dashboard**:
   ```sql
   SELECT * FROM notifications WHERE type = 'mention' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5;
   ```
2. Log in as the mentioned user — they should see a notification with the `@` icon and "Mentioned you in a post"
3. Click the notification — should navigate to the post

## 5. Edge cases to check

- Type `@` then delete it — dropdown should dismiss
- Mention yourself — no self-notification should be created
- Mention someone you **don't** follow — they shouldn't appear in autocomplete (by design)
- Empty follow list — dropdown should show nothing
- Multiple mentions in one post — each unique user gets one notification

## 6. Mobile (if you have the app running)

- Open a post that contains a mention — should render as amber-colored tappable text
- Tap it — should navigate to VisitProfile screen

## Important note

The `mention` notification type needs to exist in your `notifications` table's `type` column. If that column has a check constraint or enum, you'll need to add `'mention'` to it:

```sql
-- Check if there's a constraint on the type column:
SELECT conname, pg_get_constraintdef(oid) 
FROM pg_constraint 
WHERE conrelid = 'notifications'::regclass AND contype = 'c';
```

If there is one, update it to include `'mention'` before testing notifications.

file.content: # @Mentions Feature ## Context Users need a way to mention other users in their posts. Mentions are limited to people you follow, which prevents abuse and keeps autocomplete relevant. When mentioned, users receive a notification. Mentions are clickable links to profiles on both web and mobile. ## Implementation Steps ### Step 1: Backend — Search Following Users Endpoint New endpoint so the autocomplete can search among people the author follows. **`server/services/getService.js`** — Add `searchFollowingUsersService(userId, query, limit)`: - Query `follows` table for `following_id` where `follower_id = userId` - If no query: return all followed users (up to limit), ordered by name - If query: ILIKE search on `name` and `username` within followed user IDs, deduplicate - Returns `{ data: [{ id, name, username, image_url, badge }] }` **`server/controller/getController.js`** — Add `searchFollowingUsersController` **`server/routes/routes.js`** — Add `GET /users/following/search` (requireAuth) **`client/API/Api.js`** — Add `searchFollowingUsers(token, query, limit)` ### Step 2: MentionNode (Custom Lexical DecoratorNode) Follow the exact `ImageNode.jsx` pattern but inline. **Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionNode.jsx`**: - `DecoratorNode` subclass, type = `"mention"` - Properties: `__mentionName`, `__mentionUserId`, `__mentionUsername` - `isInline()` returns `true` (critical difference from ImageNode) - `exportJSON()` → `{ type: "mention", version: 1, mentionName, mentionUserId, mentionUsername }` - `importJSON()` → reconstructs node from serialized data - `getTextContent()` → `@username` (for word count, copy/paste) - `decorate()` → renders `` with profile navigation - `createDOM()` → `` - Export helpers: `$createMentionNode()`, `$isMentionNode()` **Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionComponent.jsx`**: - Renders `@{name}` - Click navigates to profile using `handleClickProfile` from `client/helpers/handleClicks.js` - When `isEditable=true` (in editor), clicks do nothing (no navigation while editing) ### Step 3: Register MentionNode **`client/src/App.jsx`** (line 75): Add `MentionNode` to nodes array **`client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx`** (lines 412, 588): Add `MentionNode` to both read-only LexicalComposer nodes arrays ### Step 4: MentionPlugin (Autocomplete) **Create `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/Plugins/MentionPlugin.jsx`**: - Uses `useLexicalComposerContext` (same pattern as `ImagePlugin.jsx`) - `registerUpdateListener` detects `@` trigger via regex `/@([\w-]*)$/` on text before cursor - Tracks query string after `@`, debounces API calls (200ms) - Calls `searchFollowingUsers` API to fetch matching followed users - Renders positioned dropdown (fixed position, calculated from DOM selection range) - Keyboard navigation: Arrow up/down, Enter/Tab to select, Escape to dismiss - On selection: replaces `@query` text with `MentionNode`, adds trailing space - `onMouseDown` with `preventDefault` on dropdown items to prevent editor blur **`client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/RichTextEditor.jsx`** (line 131): Add `` after `` ### Step 5: Mention Styles **`client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editor.css`** — Add: - `.mention-chip`: inline, accent color, font-weight 600, subtle hover bg - `.mention-chip--editing`: cursor default, lighter bg, no hover underline - `.mention-dropdown`: fixed position, z-index 200, glass bg, rounded, shadow, max-height 280px with overflow scroll - `.mention-dropdown-item`: flex row with avatar + name/username, hover highlight - `.mention-dropdown-item--selected`: highlighted state for keyboard navigation - Uses existing CSS vars (`--accent-purple`, `--bg-elevated`, `--border-light`, `--text-primary`, `--text-muted`) ### Step 6: Mention Notifications on Publish **Create `server/utils/extractMentions.js`** — `extractMentionUserIds(contentJson)`: - Walks Lexical JSON tree recursively - Collects unique `mentionUserId` from nodes with `type === 'mention'` - Returns array of user IDs **`server/services/uploadService.js`** — Modify `uploadJournalContentService` (line 293): - Change insert to `.select('id').single()` to get the new journal ID back - After successful insert, call `extractMentionUserIds(content)` - Batch insert notifications with `type: 'mention'`, `journal_id`, `sender_id`, `receiver_id` - Filter out self-mentions, cap at 50 notifications, non-fatal (try/catch) **`client/helpers/formatNoficationType.js`** — Add to `notificationTypeMap`: - `mention: 'Mentioned you in a post'` **`client/src/components/Notifications/notificationsCards.jsx`** — Add click handler for mention notifications: - Navigate to the post (same as like/comment behavior, using `journal_id`) ### Step 7: Mobile LexicalRenderer **`iskrib_mobile_app/apps/mobile/src/lib/content/LexicalRenderer.tsx`**: - Add `mentionName`, `mentionUserId`, `mentionUsername` to `LexicalNode` interface - Add `'mention'` case in `RenderInline`: render as `` with `accentAmber` color, `onPress` navigates to `VisitProfile` screen via `useNavigation` - Import `useNavigation` from `@react-navigation/native` ## Files to Create - `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionNode.jsx` - `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/MentionComponent.jsx` - `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/nodes/Plugins/MentionPlugin.jsx` - `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/RichTextEditor.jsx` — add MentionPlugin (line 131) - `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editor.css` — mention styles - `server/services/uploadService.js` — extract mentions + create notifications on publish - `client/helpers/formatNoficationType.js` — add 'mention' type text - `client/src/components/Notifications/notificationsCards.jsx` — mention notification click handler - `iskrib_mobile_app/apps/mobile/src/lib/content/LexicalRenderer.tsx` — mention rendering ## Key Reuse - `ImageNode.jsx` pattern → MentionNode structure (DecoratorNode, exportJSON/importJSON, decorate) - `ImagePlugin.jsx` pattern → MentionPlugin structure (useLexicalComposerContext) - `handleClickProfile` from `client/helpers/handleClicks.js` → profile navigation in MentionComponent - `searchUsersService` pattern from `server/services/getService.js` → searchFollowingUsersService (ILIKE search, dedup) - Existing notification insert pattern from `interactService.js` → mention notifications - `FormatNotificationType` map → add 'mention' entry ## Verification 1. Save post → JSON contains `"type": "mention"` nodes with userId/username 5. View post in ContentView → mention renders as clickable link, navigates to `/@username` 6. Mentioned user receives notification with "Mentioned you in a post" 7. Click mention notification → navigates to the post 8. Mobile app → mention renders as tappable amber text, tapping navigates to VisitProfile

content.text: This is a different task — debugging why mention notifications aren't being received. Let me investigate the implementation to find the root cause.

input.prompt:
I need to debug why @mention notifications aren't being received by the mentioned user. Trace the full notification flow:

1. **uploadService.js** — Read the `uploadJournalContentService` function. Look at how `extractMentionUserIds(content)` is called. The `content` parameter comes from the controller — check if it's a string or already-parsed JSON. The `extractMentionUserIds` function expects to parse it. Also check if the insert with `.select('id').single()` actually returns the journal ID properly.

2. **extractMentions.js** — Read `server/utils/extractMentions.js`. Check if the tree walking logic is correct — does it properly walk `root.children` and recurse into nested paragraph/text nodes? Remember that in Lexical JSON, mentions are inline nodes inside paragraph nodes, so the structure is `root > paragraph > mention`. Make sure the walker handles this.

3. **uploadController.js** — Read the upload controller to see how `content` is passed to the service. Check if `content` is from `req.body.content` and whether it's already parsed or still a string. Important: the route uses `multer` middleware (`upload`) which may parse form data differently.

4. **Notification table constraints** — Search for any CHECK constraints or enum restrictions on the `type` column of the `notifications` table. Look in any SQL migration files for the notifications table schema. Search for files like `*.sql` that mention `notifications` and `type`.

5. **How existing notifications work** — Read `server/services/interactService.js` to see how other notification types (like, comment, follow) insert into the notifications table. Compare the insert shape with what we're doing for mentions.

Be thorough — the most likely causes are:
- The `content` field being a string that isn't properly parsed
- The notifications table `type` column having a constraint that doesn't include 'mention'
- The journal ID not being returned from the insert
- The content JSON tree not being walked correctly (missing nested children)

1→-- hottest_tracker.sql
     2→-- Scalable #1 Hottest Post Tracker using pg_cron
     3→-- Replaces the Node.js setInterval-based tracker with a Postgres-native solution.
     4→-- Follows the same pattern as freedom_wall_cron.sql.
     5→
     6→create extension if not exists pg_cron;
     7→
     8→-- Table (idempotent — won't fail if it already exists)
     9→create table if not exists public.hottest_tracker (
    10→    id         uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
    11→    month_key  text not null unique,
    12→    journal_id uuid not null,
    13→    user_id    uuid not null,
    14→    hot_score  bigint not null default 0,
    15→    updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
    16→);
    17→
    18→-- Widen hot_score to bigint if the table already existed with integer
    19→alter table public.hottest_tracker
    20→    alter column hot_score type bigint;
    21→
    22→-- Index for fast monthly filtering of public journals
    23→create index if not exists idx_journals_privacy_created
    24→    on public.journals (privacy, created_at);
    25→
    26→-- ─── Main function ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27→create or replace function public.check_hottest_post_tracker()
    28→returns void
    29→language plpgsql
    30→security definer
    31→set search_path = public
    32→as $fn$
    33→declare
    34→    v_month_key      text;
    35→    v_month_start    timestamptz;
    36→    v_month_end      timestamptz;
    37→    v_top_journal_id uuid;
    38→    v_top_user_id    uuid;
    39→    v_top_score      bigint;
    40→    v_old_journal_id uuid;
    41→    v_old_user_id    uuid;
    42→begin
    43→    -- Prevent concurrent runs across replicas / cron overlap
    44→    perform pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext('hottest_post_tracker'));
    45→
    46→    -- Current month boundaries (UTC)
    47→    v_month_start := date_trunc('month', now() at time zone 'UTC') at time zone 'UTC';
    48→    v_month_end   := (v_month_start + interval '1 month');
    49→    v_month_key   := to_char(now() at time zone 'UTC', 'YYYY-MM');
    50→
    51→    -- Find the #1 public post this month by hot score
    52→    select
    53→        j.id,
    54→        j.user_id,
    55→        (coalesce(j.views, 0)::bigint * 6)
    56→            + (coalesce(lk.cnt, 0)::bigint * 3)
    57→            + (coalesce(cm.cnt, 0)::bigint * 2)
    58→            + (coalesce(bk.cnt, 0)::bigint * 2)
    59→    into v_top_journal_id, v_top_user_id, v_top_score
    60→    from public.journals j
    61→    left join lateral (
    62→        select count(*) as cnt from public.likes where journal_id = j.id
    63→    ) lk on true
    64→    left join lateral (
    65→        select count(*) as cnt from public.comments where post_id = j.id
    66→    ) cm on true
    67→    left join lateral (
    68→        select count(*) as cnt from public.bookmarks where journal_id = j.id
    69→    ) bk on true
    70→    where j.privacy = 'public'
    71→      and j.created_at >= v_month_start
    72→      and j.created_at <  v_month_end
    73→    order by
    74→        (coalesce(j.views, 0)::bigint * 6)
    75→            + (coalesce(lk.cnt, 0)::bigint * 3)
    76→            + (coalesce(cm.cnt, 0)::bigint * 2)
    77→            + (coalesce(bk.cnt, 0)::bigint * 2) desc,
    78→        j.created_at desc,
    79→        j.id desc
    80→    limit 1;
    81→
    82→    -- No public posts this month — nothing to do
    83→    if v_top_journal_id is null then
    84→        return;
    85→    end if;
    86→
    87→    -- Check existing tracker row for this month
    88→    select journal_id, user_id
    89→    into v_old_journal_id, v_old_user_id
    90→    from public.hottest_tracker
    91→    where month_key = v_month_key;
    92→
    93→    -- Same post is still #1 — nothing changed
    94→    if v_old_journal_id is not null and v_old_journal_id = v_top_journal_id then
    95→        return;
    96→    end if;
    97→
    98→    -- Notify old #1 that they've been replaced
    99→    -- Skip if: post was deleted, or same user's different post took over (no need to say "replaced" then "congrats")
   100→    if v_old_journal_id is not null
   101→       and v_old_user_id is distinct from v_top_user_id
   102→       and exists (select 1 from public.journals where id = v_old_journal_id)
   103→    then

   105→        values (v_old_user_id, v_old_user_id, v_old_journal_id, 'hottest_post_replaced', false);
   106→    end if;
   107→
   108→    -- Upsert new #1
   109→    insert into public.hottest_tracker (month_key, journal_id, user_id, hot_score, updated_at)
   110→    values (v_month_key, v_top_journal_id, v_top_user_id, v_top_score, now())
   111→    on conflict (month_key)
   112→    do update set
   113→        journal_id = excluded.journal_id,
   114→        user_id    = excluded.user_id,
   115→        hot_score  = excluded.hot_score,
   116→        updated_at = excluded.updated_at;
   117→
   118→    -- Notify new #1
   119→    insert into public.notifications (sender_id, receiver_id, journal_id, type, read)
   120→    values (v_top_user_id, v_top_user_id, v_top_journal_id, 'hottest_post', false);
   121→end;
